introSection: HyperSonic XLC was an S&S Worldwide launched roller coaster formerly located at Kings Dominion in Doswell, Virginia, USA. The XLC part of the name stands for "Xtreme Launched Coaster". It operated from 2001 to 2007.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
HyperSonic XLC was announced on August 1, 2000. Paramount Parks purchased a prototype from the S&S testing facility in Logan, Utah and sent it to Paramount's Kings Dominion. The ride was originally going to be located at Paramount's Great America, but the plan was cancelled. Construction of Hypersonic XLC began in October 2000 when track pieces arrived at Paramount's Kings Dominion. HyperSonic XLC opened on March 24, 2001.

HyperSonic XLC was a prototype and suffered many maintenance issues. The high g-forces would cause the tires to be crushed. In addition, too much stress was put on the track. As a result, several cracks were found in some spots of the ride.

In 2002, the ride did not operate until June 20, as the park made modifications to the ride in an attempt to improve its reliability. In 2006, the ride was listed for sale. It was ultimately removed after the 2007 season. The station and maintenance buildings were not removed, and remain to this day.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: The train was launched from 0- in 1.8 seconds, into a top hat with a 90-degree drop. The rest of the ride was a banked turn and small hill before hitting the brake run.

fullText:
HyperSonic XLC was an S&S Worldwide launched roller coaster formerly located at Kings Dominion in Doswell, Virginia, USA. The XLC part of the name stands for "Xtreme Launched Coaster". It operated from 2001 to 2007.

HyperSonic XLC was announced on August 1, 2000. Paramount Parks purchased a prototype from the S&S testing facility in Logan, Utah and sent it to Paramount's Kings Dominion. The ride was originally going to be located at Paramount's Great America, but the plan was cancelled. Construction of Hypersonic XLC began in October 2000 when track pieces arrived at Paramount's Kings Dominion. HyperSonic XLC opened on March 24, 2001.

HyperSonic XLC was a prototype and suffered many maintenance issues. The high g-forces would cause the tires to be crushed. In addition, too much stress was put on the track. As a result, several cracks were found in some spots of the ride.

In 2002, the ride did not operate until June 20, as the park made modifications to the ride in an attempt to improve its reliability. In 2006, the ride was listed for sale. It was ultimately removed after the 2007 season. The station and maintenance buildings were not removed, and remain to this day.

The train was launched from 0- in 1.8 seconds, into a top hat with a 90-degree drop. The rest of the ride was a banked turn and small hill before hitting the brake run.

White track and supports.
